data.title: A dormant address holding 300 BTC has become active again after nearly 11 years
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to Whale Alert monitoring, a dormant address containing 300 BTC (US$29,368,562) was just activated after 10.9 years.

data.title: Dormant Bitcoin Address Activated After Over A Decade
data.description: According to Odaily, a dormant Bitcoin address containing 300 BTC, valued at approximately $29,368,562, has been activated after remaining inactive for 10.9 years. This activity was detected by Whale Alert, a service that monitors large cryptocurrency transactions.

data.title: A certain address bought $2.5 million of LIBRA and lost $2.1 million after liquidation
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News The Data Nerd monitored that 5 hours ago, address 62KQa bought 659,000 LIBRA at a price of 2.5 million US dollars (average entry price of 3.79 US dollars). One hour later, he sold all at a price of 392,000 US dollars, with an actual loss of about 2.1 million US dollars (investment return rate -84.32%).

data.title: Solayer founder has announced the first member of the team behind LIBRA
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Solayer founder Chaofan Shou published the personal information of the first member of the international sales director of KIP Protocol, the team behind LIBRA, on the X platform.
Earlier news said that Solayer founder and engineer lost more than 2 million US dollars due to LIBRA and said they would take action.

data.title: Goldman Sachs: Only holds $3.6 million worth of GBTC funds, and the market value of GBTC has dropped to about $19.5 billion
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to regulatory documents submitted to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, with the increase in holdings of BlackRock and Fidelity Bitcoin ETFs, Goldman Sachs currently only holds Grayscale Bitcoin Trust Fund GBTC worth $3.6 million. According to official data from Grayscale, as of February 14, GBTC's Bitcoin holdings have dropped to about 200,000 (200,118.5305 BTC), with a market value of $19.45 billion. (Cointelegraph (

data.title: Ray Dalio Discusses Bitcoin And Currency Depreciation
data.description:
According to PANews, Ray Dalio, founder of Bridgewater Associates, shared his views on Bitcoin and the DeepSeek trend during a recent 'All-In' podcast interview. Dalio observed that currencies are depreciating, particularly against assets like gold, Bitcoin, and other tangible assets. He noted that fiat currencies are generally losing value, while hard assets are appreciating.

Dalio mentioned that Bitcoin and other digital assets are significant topics of discussion. While commodities can hedge against inflation, they lack the ability to serve as long-term stores of value, unlike gold or Bitcoin. He revealed that he still holds some Bitcoin as part of a diversified investment strategy but prefers gold due to its unique advantages.

Gold, according to Dalio, can move freely between countries, is held by central banks as a reserve asset, and is more challenging to regulate, allowing for more private ownership. Additionally, gold is not easily taxed, unlike Bitcoin, which is subject to regulatory scrutiny and taxation due to its traceable transaction paths.

data.title: Las Vegas man accused of running $24M crypto-linked Ponzi scheme
data.description:
Brent Kovar allegedly attracted investors to the Ponzi scheme through a YouTube video and PowerPoint presentation, promising annual returns of 15% to 30%.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/las-vegas-man-crypto-linked-ai-ponzi-scheme-charges?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound
